seoTitle: How to Link Google Calendar With GoHighLevel
metaDescription: Connect Google Calendar to GoHighLevel so bookings, appointments, and availability stay synced automatically between the two tools.
intro: Running appointments through GoHighLevel only works well if your real calendar reflects what's actually booked. Linking Google Calendar closes that gap, keeping your availability accurate and preventing double-bookings. Here's how to set up the connection.

steps.heading: Go to your GoHighLevel calendar settings
steps.body: Open the Calendars section inside your GoHighLevel sub-account, where third-party calendar connections are managed.

steps.heading: Start the Google Calendar connection
steps.body: Choose the option to connect a Google account and sign in with the Google account you want to sync.

steps.heading: Grant calendar permissions
steps.body: Approve the requested permissions so GoHighLevel can read and write events to keep both calendars aligned.

steps.heading: Choose sync direction and default calendar
steps.body: Select which Google calendar should sync with GoHighLevel and confirm whether bookings should push both ways.

steps.heading: Test with a sample booking
steps.body: Book a test appointment in GoHighLevel and confirm it appears on Google Calendar, and vice versa, before relying on it live.

faq.question: Does linking Google Calendar cost extra in GoHighLevel?
faq.answer: The calendar integration itself is part of GoHighLevel's core calendar feature, not a separate paid add-on.

faq.question: Can I connect more than one Google Calendar?
faq.answer: Yes, GoHighLevel lets you connect multiple calendars to different users or booking pages within an account.

faq.question: What happens if I disconnect the calendar later?
faq.answer: Disconnecting stops future syncing, but events already created generally remain on both platforms.

faq.question: Will existing Google Calendar events block new bookings?
faq.answer: Once synced, GoHighLevel checks your Google Calendar availability so existing events prevent conflicting bookings.
